title: Supersede 'inversion' language with 'rotation' in 2026-05-17 Concepts that predate axis-rotation correction
description:
Substrate-doctrine debt from this session. Multiple Concepts in vaults/wrkstrm-doctrine/ + cross-vault index edits use 'inversion' language to describe substrate's relationship to PG/Meta/consumer-tech doctrines. Per the axis-rotation-across-cognitive-vector-space Concept (committed at 5b571470b3 as part of the doctrine vault seed), this framing is wrong — substrate doctrines are multidimensional axis ROTATIONS in cognitive Hilbert space, not binary inversions. 

Do not silent-edit the prior Concepts (substrate doctrine: supersession-not-edit). Instead, author refinement Concepts that supersede the framing layer, leaving the original Concepts intact with their pre-correction prose AND a forward-pointing `supersededBy`-equivalent reference to the rotation-framed refinement.

Concepts that need rotation-framed refinement supersession:
- arm-fluff-for-substrate-content (uses 'inversion' implicitly in pros/cons)
- engage-politics-via-typed-rendering (frames as 'opposite of PG's ban')
- bastards-for-peace-and-love (uses 'inversion' in cons)
- dick-pick-pop-culture-semantic-bridge (uses 'inversion' implicitly)
- mass-cultural-surface-for-substantive-core (uses 'inversion' implicitly)
- substrate-externalizes-google-test-harness-database-moat (uses 'inverts' in pros)

Also edit (not via supersession — these are metadata, not typed Concepts):
- vaults/wrkstrm-doctrine/index.md — replace 'inversion' language with 'rotation' in cross-vault opposition table
- vaults/wrkstrm-doctrine/receipts/2026-05-17-substrate-doctrine-capture.json — update prose if needed
- vaults/paulgraham/index.md — replace 'inversion' in cross-vault section

Closure: 6+ supersession Concepts authored; index.md edits landed; LDT proof per substrate doctrine asserting the rotation-framed Concepts correctly cite the inversion-framed Concepts via sp chain.
